mechanism_narrative: A high absolute level of the Federal Funds Effective Rate indicates restrictive monetary policy, which increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust and expanding economy, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity market returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: A signal based on the 3-month change in PAYEMS fails to achieve a positive Information Coefficient (IC) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer demand is strong (rising RSAFS) but inflation is also high (elevated CPIAUCSL), the market anticipates aggressive Fed tightening to curb demand-pull inflation, causing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS momentum and CPIAUCSL level is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a regression predicting 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a very low unemployment rate signals maximum employment, portending wage inflation and subsequent monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are not statistically significantly lower when UNRATE is below 4.7% compared to when it is above, conditional on being in an expansion.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates a healthy supply side of the economy, translating to strong corporate revenue growth and positive short-term equity market sentiment.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in INDPRO has a rolling 24-month correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns that is not consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in housing starts is a leading indicator of a broad-based slowdown in consumer and construction-related demand, negatively affecting forward-looking earnings and equity prices.
failure_condition: Instances of HOUST falling more than 10% over 3 months are not followed by a negative average 60-day S&P 500 return over the subsequent period.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real returns and forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance, leading to a compression of equity multiples over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the level of CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong payroll growth (high PAYEMS change) and a low unemployment rate (low UNRATE) signals an overheating labor market, prompting fears of wage-price spirals and Fed intervention, thus negatively impacting equities.
failure_condition: A strategy that shorts the S&P 500 when PAYEMS 3-month growth is in the top quartile and UNRATE is in the bottom quartile fails to generate a positive alpha.
